The experience was great.

Located right opposite the Churchgate subway, this lovely cafe looked very inviting  indeed. Walking in it felt quite fancy compared to any Starbucks or Blue Tokai. The ambience and decor is also nice, but maybe the tables are a bit too close to each other, which causes you to overhear conversations even if you don't want to. 
There is hardly any interaction with the staff whatsoever cause you order through their QR code and they'll bring it to your table when it's ready. Once you are done eating you just call for the bill and it's done. Let's get on with the food, which was the main source of disappointment.
Starting with the SPAGHETTI AGLIO OLIO, the most bland pasta I've ever had! I was sad that the portion size looked quite small, not at all value for money, but one bite of it and I was glad I won't have to eat a lot of this tasteless mess, which is even worse! Maybe this cafe caters to more NRI public and thus they might be holding on to the spice, but boy there was none of it! I am glad that me and my partner both were hungry, that's the only reason we finished the pasta. Guys, you need to rework on this!
Next came the ORANGE SPARK MOCKTAIL. It was a decent beverage yes, and it certainly had more masala than the spaghetti! But those huge cubes (or more like cylinders) of ice were a spoil sport. But still a decent drink nevertheless.
We had hoped this would fill us up and so ordered the dessert first-NUTELLA FLUFFY PANCAKE. a stack of 4-5 small to average sized pancakes drizzled with an average amount of Nutella and a size portion of whipped cream. Yes, it was good. Yes, I might eat it again. No, I wouldn't go to this place just to eat this.
Realising that we are still hungry, we went over budget and ordered a very expensive sounding sandwich. Oh the HAM & CHEESE BRIOCHE was goood! It had a lovely proportion of meat and cheese, with a side of nachos and salad. Yup, the sandwich was expensive but worth it! Though the nachos were tasteless, with no masala, no spices, just crunch! 
Honestly the experience was not that good, but the sandwich kind of made up for it. Spaghetti was seriously not worth it. I just realised, the name of the place is Coffee Island and we didn't have their coffee. How ironic!
Happy Burping!!!
